stem: You have had your driving licence for 3 years. What is the legal alcohol limit for you in the Netherlands?

choices.text: 0.2 promille
0.5 promille
0.8 promille
There is no limit if you feel fine

explanation.short: Drivers with less than 5 years' experience are limited to 0.2 promille.
explanation.examTip: After 5 years the general limit of 0.5 promille applies.

stem: Your medicine has a yellow warning sticker on the box. What does this mean for driving?

choices.text: It is safe to drive at any time
The medicine can affect your driving — do not drive
You may only drive within the city
You may drive if you take half the dose

explanation.short: A yellow sticker warns the medicine impairs driving; do not drive while taking it.
explanation.examTip: Check the package leaflet or ask your pharmacist if you are unsure.

stem: You feel sleepy on a long drive. What is the safest action?

choices.text: Open a window and drive faster
Turn up the music and keep going
Take a break of at least 15 minutes, with coffee or fresh air
Take stronger painkillers to feel alert

explanation.short: A real break is the only effective answer to tiredness.
explanation.examTip: Fatigue impairs reaction time as much as alcohol — never drive on through it.

stem: For economical driving in a petrol car, you should generally shift up:

choices.text: Around 1000 rpm
Around 2000 rpm
Around 3500 rpm
Only at the red line

explanation.short: Shifting up around 2000 rpm in a petrol car (1500 rpm in diesel) is the eco-driving sweet spot.
explanation.examTip: Looking far ahead lets you roll smoothly instead of braking and accelerating.

stem: You expect to wait at a closed level crossing for more than a minute. To save fuel you should:

choices.text: Keep the engine running in neutral
Switch the engine off
Rev the engine occasionally
Switch on all lights

explanation.short: If you expect to wait longer than about a minute, switch the engine off.
explanation.examTip: Many modern cars do this automatically with a start-stop system.

stem: Which of these increases fuel consumption?

choices.text: Correctly inflated tyres
A clean, empty boot
Underinflated tyres and a roof box you do not need
Shifting up early

explanation.short: Low tyre pressure and unnecessary loads both raise consumption.
explanation.examTip: Check tyre pressure at least once a month.

stem: Your car has broken down on the shoulder of a motorway. Where do you place the warning triangle?

choices.text: Directly behind the car
About 30 metres behind the car
About 100 metres behind the car
On the roof of the car

explanation.short: On a motorway, place the triangle about 100 metres behind the vehicle.
explanation.examTip: On normal roads 30 metres is enough; always put on a high-visibility vest first.

stem: You arrive first at the scene of an accident with injuries. What do you do first?

choices.text: Move the injured people to the side of the road
Drive on; someone else will help
Make the area safe, then call 112
Take photos for insurance before doing anything else

explanation.short: Secure the scene with hazards and triangle, then call 112.
explanation.examTip: Only move injured people if they are in immediate danger (fire, traffic).

stem: After a minor collision with a parked car whose owner is not present, you must:

choices.text: Drive on if the damage looks small
Leave a note with your name, address and registration, and inform the police if needed
Wait only 5 minutes and then leave
Repair the other car yourself

explanation.short: You must always identify yourself after causing damage — leave your details if the owner is absent.
explanation.examTip: Driving away from any collision is a criminal offence (doorrijden na ongeval).

stem: An ambulance approaches from behind with siren and blue lights. You should:

choices.text: Brake hard immediately
Pull aside to the right and stop if necessary, making a clear path
Speed up so it does not have to overtake
Stay in the left lane to keep right clear

explanation.short: Make space to the right; stop if needed without blocking junctions.
explanation.examTip: Emergency vehicles with siren and blue lights always have priority.

stem: Which is the correct emergency number for police, fire and ambulance in the Netherlands?

explanation.short: 112 reaches all emergency services and is free from any phone.
explanation.examTip: Start with the location, then describe what has happened.

stem: When helping at an accident, what is the most important thing to do before approaching the victims?

choices.text: Check the damage to the cars
Make the scene safe and protect yourself (hazards, vest, triangle)
Take statements from witnesses
Move the vehicles off the road

explanation.short: Secure the scene first — an unsafe helper quickly becomes a second casualty.
explanation.examTip: Keep injured people still and warm until professional help arrives.

stem: What is the blood-alcohol limit for an experienced Dutch driver (more than 5 years licensed)?

explanation.short: Experienced drivers: 0.5 ‰ (0.22 mg/L breath). Novice drivers in their first 5 years: 0.2 ‰.
explanation.examTip: When in doubt — don't drive. Any alcohol slows reactions and judgement.

stem: Which is an early warning sign that you are too tired to keep driving?

choices.text: Yawning, drifting in the lane, missing exits
Bright sunlight
An empty fuel tank
Hot weather alone

explanation.short: Yawning, drifting, eye-strain and lapses in concentration mean it is time to stop.
explanation.examTip: Open windows and loud music are not real cures — take a real break.

stem: Your car breaks down on the hard shoulder of a motorway. After stopping safely, what is the next step?

choices.text: Stay in the car with seat belt on
Put on hazard lights, place the warning triangle ~30 m behind, get everyone out and behind the safety barrier
Open the bonnet and start checking the engine
Walk back along the carriageway to warn drivers

explanation.short: Hazards on, warning triangle behind the car, everyone out on the verge behind the barrier — then call for help.
explanation.examTip: Use the emergency lane only as a last resort; never stop there for non-emergency reasons.

stem: You are first at a road accident where someone is unconscious. What is the most important first action?

choices.text: Move the victim out of the car immediately
Make the scene safe, then call 112 and check if the person is breathing
Take photos for the insurance
Drive on so as not to block traffic

explanation.short: Secure the scene (hazards, warning triangle), call 112, then assess breathing and airway.
explanation.examTip: Move a casualty only when there is an immediate further danger such as fire.
